reasoning: The proxy value for April 27 is 4.72 (from Friday, April 25), representing a sharp plunge from recent highs around 4.87 earlier in the week due to tariff/recession fears. In the short term (May 4), mean reversion from this local trough is highly likely, supported by the 30-day linear trend pointing to ~4.88. As the forecast horizon extends to May 27 and beyond, negative drift takes over: Reuters consensus and broader macroeconomic outlooks predict 10-year Treasury yields will decline toward 4.2% by mid-to-late 2025 as the Fed continues to cut rates amid economic slowing. This will likely pull AAA corporate yields slightly below the 4.72 threshold over the longer term. However, because uncertainty strictly increases with time—incorporating unpredictable geopolitical shocks and inflation fluctuations—the long-term probabilities have been strategically decayed back toward 0.5, ensuring they reflect the widest possible distribution of outcomes at extended horizons.
